title: Rich Middle Eastern Lamb Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful Middle Eastern dish featuring tender lamb, aromatic spices, and a deep, slightly sweet flavor profile, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This Whole30-friendly recipe is made with wholesome ingredients and is free from grains, dairy, and added sugars.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove the lamb from the pot and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Stir in the ground cumin, smoked paprika, and black pepper. Cook for 1 minute.
Add the chopped dried apricots and prunes to the pot, stirring to combine.
Return the lamb to the pot and add enough water to cover the ingredients. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Season the tagine with salt to taste, then serve hot over cauliflower rice or roasted vegetables.
Garnish with fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
Let the tagine cool completely before refrigerating or freezing for later use.
